questions.question: What is agriculture?
questions.correct_answer: The practice of cultivating crops and raising animals for food and raw materials
questions.explanation: Agriculture is a primary economic activity that supports food production and industries.

questions.question: What is primitive subsistence farming?
questions.correct_answer: A traditional farming method practiced on small land using simple tools and family labour
questions.explanation: It often involves shifting cultivation and depends on natural soil fertility and rainfall.

questions.question: What is shifting cultivation?
questions.correct_answer: A farming method where land is cleared by cutting and burning vegetation and cultivated for a short time
questions.explanation: After soil fertility decreases, farmers move to another piece of land.

questions.question: What is intensive subsistence farming?
questions.correct_answer: A farming method where farmers use small land areas intensively to produce high yields
questions.explanation: It uses large amounts of labour and modern inputs such as fertilizers and irrigation.

questions.question: What is commercial farming?
questions.correct_answer: Farming where crops are grown mainly for sale in markets
questions.explanation: It involves the use of modern technology, fertilizers, and mechanized equipment.

questions.question: What are the three main cropping seasons in India?
questions.explanation: These seasons depend on climatic conditions and determine the time crops are grown.

questions.question: What are Rabi crops?
questions.correct_answer: Crops grown in winter and harvested in summer
questions.explanation: Examples include wheat, barley, peas, gram, and mustard.

questions.question: What are Kharif crops?
questions.correct_answer: Crops grown during the monsoon season
questions.explanation: Examples include rice, maize, cotton, jute, and groundnut.

questions.question: What is plantation agriculture?
questions.correct_answer: A type of commercial farming where a single crop is grown on large estates
questions.explanation: Plantation crops require large capital investment and labour.

questions.question: What was the Green Revolution?
questions.correct_answer: An agricultural movement that increased crop production using modern technology
questions.explanation: It introduced high-yielding seeds, fertilizers, irrigation, and mechanization.

notes.title: Agriculture
notes.content: Agriculture is one of the most important primary activities and provides livelihood to a large population in India.

notes.title: Types of Farming
notes.content: Important farming types include primitive subsistence farming, intensive subsistence farming, and commercial farming.

notes.title: Cropping Seasons
notes.content: India has three cropping seasons: Rabi (winter), Kharif (monsoon), and Zaid (summer).

notes.title: Plantation Crops
notes.content: Tea, coffee, rubber, and sugarcane are examples of plantation crops grown on large estates.

notes.title: Agricultural Reforms
notes.content: Reforms such as land reforms and the Green Revolution improved agricultural productivity.

facts.fact: India is one of the world's largest producers of rice and wheat.
Rice is the staple food crop of a majority of the population in India.
Plantation agriculture usually focuses on a single crop grown over a large area.
The Green Revolution significantly increased food grain production in India.
